author: @lisakingscott7729
text:
I bought a load of this a couple of years ago. Amazing stuff...
In a spray it's brilliant to remove soot deposits from a limestone fireplace.
It works excellently in my carpet / hard floor / upholstery cleaner. I spray stubborn stains with the solution initially and scrub for good measure, before going over with the cleaner. It's actually removed stains from a carpet a professional company gave up on!
However I find it dries my hands and is not antimicrobial. So I made a couple of mods, when I use it as a hand cleaner. To overcome the skin dryness I added some baby oil, which doesn't mix well, but a quick shake before using it creates a great emulsion and foams well.
For antimicrobial action I added a few drops of tea tree oil. This has antiviral and antibacterial action in low doses. It's not been tested on new variant Coronavirus, so I make no claims, but it kills all other viruses it's been tested on at very low doses. Note that some people are allergic to tea tree and it can be toxic.
BTW, I also added a few drops of tea tree to my alcohol spray, to give longer lasting antimicrobial action on my hands!

author: @johnstockwellmajorsmedleyb1214
text:
You sir are a damn genius and I wish I was in the UK. I would gladly work at any price to just learn directly from you. My mind works similarly though with wood, metal, re-purposing, remodeling, design/drawing, installation, and automotive. I am blown away since finding your channel. Things I had often wondered are the things you so effectively cover on your channel. Amazing sir absolutely amazing, and commendable as it is obvious like me you are not at all afraid to get your personal hand tools filthy!!! 


I/we possess a skill set 98% of the population wishes they had
My craft allows me to build or repair anything anywhere in the world
The last of a dying breed of free thinkers and driven doers 

The last of those willing to get their hands dirty in pursuit of ideas 

Never satisfied with good enough we fail 99% of the time 

Driven to achieve despite adversity that 1% being successful
Then quickly on to the next idea with the knowledge all the others have provided
